data.title: DBS CEO's direct stake rises to 1.4 million shares via vesting
data.description:
About 23,379 shares were vested at no cash consideration on 16 February.

DBS Group Holdings Ltd CEO Tan Su Shan has increased her direct shareholding in the bank to 1,415,991 ordinary shares, or 0.05%, following the vesting of share awards.

About 23,379 shares were vested at no cash consideration on 16 February, raising her direct interest from 1,392,612 shares to 1,415,991, according to a bourse filing.

Earlier, 40,681 shares were vested on 13 February.

Separately, on 12 February, Tan was granted 81,099 awards of fully paid ordinary shares under the DBSH Share Plan.

Following the events, her rights and options over shares decreased from 360,604 to 296,544.

data.title: AIA Singapore’s payout hits $40b in last decade
data.description:
Claims and maturity proceeds span protection and savings.

AIA Singapore paid out more than $40b in death, critical illness, accident, hospitalisation and maturity proceeds over the last decade, the company said in a press release marking its 95th anniversary.

The $40b covers protection claims and maturity payouts to policyholders over the last 10 years, including payments linked to retirement and savings products.

The insurer said it safeguards the health and wellness of more than one-third of Singapore’s workforce and was the first insurer in Singapore to provide mental health coverage to policyholders.

AIA Singapore also operates the AIA+ super app integrating insurance, healthcare, wellness and financial management services, and said AIA Vitality members enjoyed over $10m worth of partner rewards and benefits in 2025.

data.title: Mencast Holdings to divest Penjuru Road property in $21m deal
data.description:
The sale is expected to yield a $7.7m gain and repay loans.

Mencast Holdings Ltd has granted an option to sell its leasehold industrial property at 42B Penjuru Road for $21m, according to a bourse filing dated 16 February.

The option was granted by the company’s wholly owned subsidiary, Mencast Marine Pte Ltd, to Grandwoods Trading (Singapore) Pte Ltd.

Grandwoods Trading has paid an option fee of $210,000 plus goods and services tax (GST). Upon exercise, it will pay a balance deposit of $840,000 plus GST, with the remaining consideration due on completion.

The option is valid until 4 pm on 18 March and, if exercised, will form a binding contract for the disposal. If not exercised, the option fee will be forfeited.

The property is a 30-year JTC leasehold industrial site commencing 1 March 2011.

The facility comprises a single-user general industrial factory with a four-storey ancillary office building, with a land area of about 16,200 square metres and gross floor area of about 13,825 square metres.

It is currently used for the group’s propulsion manufacturing operations and will be sold subject to JTC’s approval for the assignment of the lease.

Based on the group’s unaudited consolidated financial statements for the half year ended 30 June 2025, the property had a net carrying amount of about $12.27m.

The disposal is expected to result in a gain of about $7.73m after deducting estimated relocation, professional and related expenses of about $1m.

Net proceeds of about $20m are intended to be used primarily to repay existing loans secured over the property, with any remaining proceeds applied towards repayment of other borrowings under the group’s debt restructuring arrangements.

data.title: Tiong Seng warns of FY2025 net loss as legacy project costs bite
data.description:
The warning follows a $2.8m net profit in FY2024.

Tiong Seng Holdings Limited is expecting to report a net loss for the financial year ended 31 December 2025 (FY 2025), following a net profit of $2.8m in FY2024, according to a bourse filing dated 16 February.

The expected loss is primarily due to lower revenue and timing differences between the recognition of variable order costs and the corresponding revenue, which remains subject to customer assessment and approval.

The Group also recognised final losses from legacy projects secured before the Covid-19 pandemic, largely due to increased materials, labour and subcontracting costs incurred and recognised in the first half of FY2025.

Tiong Seng will release further details of the group’s performance when it publishes its unaudited FY2025 results by 1 March 2026.

data.title: CSE Global FY2025 new orders hit $1b as Q4 electrification triples
data.description:
The group closed the year with an order book of $709.5m.

CSE Global Limited hit $1b in new orders for FY2025, up 28.3% from $800.7m in FY2024, driven by growth in its electrification and communications segments.

The growth included an extension of an existing contract with a hyperscaler customer and major electrification contracts in the US liquefied natural gas (LNG) market.

The group closed FY2025 with an order book of $709.5m, it said in a bourse filing.

It secured $514.7m of new orders in the fourth quarter ended 31 December 2025 (Q4 2025), a 118.7% rise from $235.3m a year earlier, on the back of stronger demand for electrification solutions in the US.

The electrification business segment led fourth-quarter growth, surging 196.4% year-on-year to $414.5m, or about 80.5% of total order intake.

Notable contracts included four US projects worth $348m for the design and manufacture of power distribution centres, integration of electrical and control systems for the LNG market, and power management systems for data centres.

Meanwhile, the communications segment contributed $55.2m, or 10.7% of total intake, in Q4 2025.

The automation segment secured $45.1m, up 13.3% from $39.8m in Q4 2024, accounting for 8.8% of orders.

The company said the developments are not expected to materially affect net tangible assets per share or earnings per share, and none of its directors or substantial shareholders has an interest in the contracts.

data.title: True 5G Standalone networks deliver 52% speed boost over hybrid 5G
data.description:
New Ookla data shows 5G SA achieves low latency by using a full 5G core.

5G Standalone (SA) networks, which operate on a fully independent 5G core rather than relying on existing 4G infrastructure, are delivering significantly higher speeds and lower latency than non-standalone 5G networks, according to a report by Ookla, the operator of Speedtest, and Omdia, a market research firm.

The report found that median download speeds on 5G SA networks were approximately 52% higher than non-standalone 5G globally.

Standalone networks also showed lower latency, which affects the responsiveness of applications such as cloud gaming, video conferencing, and industrial connectivity.

Unlike non-standalone 5G networks, which depend on 4G cores, standalone networks can support features such as network slicing and enterprise-grade connectivity.

These capabilities allow operators to offer dedicated network services for industries and other specific use cases.

Despite the performance benefits, 5G SA networks still account for a small share of global 5G connections with the report stating standalone networks represented 17.6% of 5G connections worldwide as of late 2025.

Deployment rates vary across regions with the report highlighting that countries including China, India, and several in the Middle East have a higher proportion of standalone networks.

In contrast, standalone adoption in some European markets remained low, with certain countries reporting rates as low as 2%.

The report also notes that the global 5G market is transitioning from initial deployments toward broader implementation of standalone networks.

data.title: DBS pilots Visa Intelligent Commerce for agent-initiated payments
data.description:
Pilot tests AI agents completing everyday purchases using issuer-controlled payment flows.

DBS has become the first issuer in the Asia Pacific to pilot Visa Intelligent Commerce, advancing real-world agent-initiated payments using AI agents.

The joint pilot with Visa aims to enable safe, transparent, and consent-driven payments by AI agents on behalf of consumers, using Visa’s secure infrastructure, integrated application programming interfaces, and partner programme.

The banks plan to expand trials to other use cases such as online shopping and travel bookings.

The study also found that eight in 10 Singapore consumers rely on AI assistance when shopping online.

As part of the pilot, DBS is validating AI-ready credentials, advanced authentication, and intent-driven transaction controls to support secure and scalable agent-led commerce.

data.title: Daily Markets Briefing: STI up 0.02%; Top stock is Jardine Matheson
data.description:
Jardine Matheson is the top stock with a 2.55% increase.

The Straits Times Index (STI) closed at 4,938.580, up 0.02% on Monday, 16 February, at 5:21 p.m.

Jardine Matheson was the most active stock, followed by Keppel Ltd. (+1.98%) and Yangzijiang Shipbuilding (Holdings) Pte Ltd (+1.17%).

UOL Group Limited and Singapore Telecommunications Limited were at the foot.

data.title: Local retail investors outpace global peers in discipline and diversification: study
data.description:
Gen Z investors allocate capital monthly, outperforming both Gen X and Baby Boomers in the city-state.

Singaporean retail investors are considered more mature compared to their global counterparts, with their high levels of engagement, disciplined portfolio construction, and greater diversification and macroeconomic awareness.

The latest quarterly Retail Investor Beat from eToro saw that 79% of 1,000 local retail investors surveyed said they are actively reviewing their investments and 83% investing regularly every month, versus 70% and 79% of retail investors globally. 

Amongst generations, the younger ones show the highest rates of active engagement. The study said that 92% of Gen Z and 88% of millennials allocate capital into the markets each month, compared to 78% and 79% of Gen X and boomers, respectively.

Singaporeans also allocate 10% of their monthly income to investing on average, higher than the 8% recorded globally.

“There’s definitely a strong awareness around compounding, making each dollar count, or just simply ‘maximising the ROI’ for every dollar. When those habits carry into adulthood, investing regularly and spreading money across different assets feels like a natural extension of how finances have always been managed,” said eToro Market Analyst Zavier Wong.

The eToro survey also showed that Singaporean retail investors are broadening their portfolios across asset classes more than retail investors globally. The number of local investors holding domestic and foreign equities, real estate, domestic and foreign bonds, cash assets and currencies, beats the global retail investor average.

When it comes to macroeconomic awareness, eToro said the city-state’s retail investors are reassessing the role of traditional defensive assets, with 63% saying they have already adjusted or plan to adjust their portfolios as the US dollar weakens.

“Retail investors in Singapore engage with macro shifts in a very practical manner. Currency movements matter to them because they sit close to their everyday financial decisions,” Wong said.

“However, what really stands out is how diversified Singaporean investors already are – far more than the global average across most asset classes – which gives them more flexibility when macro conditions shift,” the expert added.

Since they have better exposure across different asset classes and geographies, Singaporean investors are better positioned to reassess traditional defensive plays as the US dollar weakens, Wong said.

data.title: ISCA sets aside $3m as 98% foresee industry disruption
data.description:
This will fund AI capability, career transition and sustainability support.

Institute of Singapore Chartered Accountants (ISCA) has set aside $3m to support artificial intelligence (AI) capability, career transition and sustainability initiatives, following survey findings showing that 98% of respondents expect disruption from global political and trade developments, according to a press release.

The institute will allocate $1m to roll out an AI Fluency Programme and $2m towards a Career Support Programme in 2025.

Survey findings conducted with the Association of Small & Medium Enterprises showed that 98% of respondents anticipate disruption, whilst 57% identified job displacement as their top AI-related concern.

The AI Fluency Programme, delivered in partnership with the Infocomm Media Development Authority under the expanded TechSkills Accelerator, will be offered free to Singaporean and permanent resident professional accountants.

ISCA said the initiative targets Singapore’s 120,000-strong accountancy sector and focuses on applying AI in audit and reporting workflows, understanding its limitations, and ensuring responsible use.

ISCA has committed $2m in 2025 to provide career coaching, job matching and skills support under its Career Support Programme, alongside mid-career measures and improvements to the SkillsFuture Level-Up Programme announced in Budget 2026.

On sustainability, ISCA said it will issue implementation guidance and reference materials to support firms adapting to revised climate reporting timelines, alongside extended green financing and energy efficiency schemes.
